title: Buyer creates an imports PO — imports flag triggers customs/landed-cost requirements
user_persona: International procurement buyer importing goods from overseas

conversation.user: Create an imports PO from supplier SUP-INTL-DE in EUR. Goods being shipped from Germany to our India warehouse.

bot_must: note Imports splice triggers additional required slots and exchange-rate fetch
ask for incoterm (e.g., FOB, CIF, DDP) and shipping mode (air/sea/road)

conversation.user: FOB Hamburg, sea freight, country DE.

bot_must: note that supplier currency EUR != likely base currency — exchange rate fetch needed (this maps to the SP IF @sup_currency != @currencycode)
fetch current EUR exchange rate via the auto-triggered SP path

conversation.user: Buyer BUY-INTL, numbering PO/IMP/2026, today, ship-to WH-MAIN. Line: ITM-IMP-7, 500 units at 22 EUR.

bot_must: compute total in EUR and base currency using fetched rate
PO created with imports flag, incoterm, country recorded

evaluation_metrics.imports_flag_detection: imports recognised from 'imports PO' or 'shipped from <country>' phrasing
evaluation_metrics.computed_splice: exchange_rate_fetch triggered automatically because currency != base
